subject: Geography
title: New Zealand's Southern Alps
description: A 60-second lesson on New Zealand's Southern Alps.

hook.text: New Zealand's Southern Alps rise because the Pacific plate is shoving under the island.

buildup.text: Aoraki/Mount Cook reaches 3,724 meters—the tallest peak in Australasia.

discovery.text: Franz Josef Glacier descends into temperate rainforest, a rare combination on Earth.

twist.text: The mountains split New Zealand's climate: wet jungle to the west, dry plains to the east.

climax.text: Peter Jackson filmed Middle-earth here because the mountains looked otherworldly.

punchline.text: Mountains so dramatic they became Middle-earth.

quiz.question: What famous film franchise used New Zealand's Southern Alps?
